metadata.title: Support Operations Health
notes: Synthetic dataset designed to feel realistic rather than represent a real company.
Fresh seed on every GitHub Pages deploy creates small but plausible movement.

alerts.title: Backlog elevated
alerts.detail: Ending backlog is 7529 tickets, above the historical median.

alerts.title: Email channel is materially slower than chat
alerts.detail: Average first response is 10.8 hours slower on email than chat.

alerts.title: Backlog is a leading indicator for SLA risk
alerts.detail: Correlation between backlog and next-day SLA breach is 0.85.

hypotheses.title: Higher backlog predicts next-day SLA breaches
hypotheses.evidence: Lag correlation between backlog and next-day breach rate is 0.85.

hypotheses.title: Email is slower and less loved than chat
hypotheses.evidence: Email is 10.8 hours slower; chat CSAT is 13.3 points higher.

hypotheses.title: Weekends create a Monday spike
hypotheses.evidence: Mondays show 21.1% more created tickets than the non-Monday average.

hypotheses.title: APAC carries the sharpest backlog burden
hypotheses.evidence: APAC average backlog exceeds the other regions by 648.2 tickets.

narrative: The operation is mostly healthy, but backlog remains the best leading indicator of service failure.
Chat performs best on both speed and satisfaction, while email is the main drag on service quality.
The operating model looks slightly under-staffed around weekends, especially in APAC.
